# Purpose
This code is a C++ header file that declares four weak functions related to event handling in a system. The functions [`event_put`](<#event_put>), [`event_put_from_app`](<#event_put_from_app>), [`event_put_from_process`](<#event_put_from_process>), and [`event_reset_from_process_queue`](<#event_reset_from_process_queue>) are defined with the `WEAK` attribute, which allows them to be overridden by other implementations if needed. These functions take parameters of types `PebbleEvent` and `PebbleTask`, which are likely defined in the included headers `kernel/events.h` and `util/attributes.h`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on event management within a specific context, possibly for a kernel or embedded system. The use of weak linkage suggests that these functions serve as default implementations that can be customized in other parts of the program.
